I let ChazGPT create a postcredit scene for The Equalizer and John Wick
FADE IN:

INT. SMALL DINER - NIGHT

In a nearly empty, dimly lit, roadside diner, ROBERT MCCALL (Denzel Washington), sits alone, nursing a cup of coffee. The waitress, a young girl with a bright smile, delivers a piece of pie and refills his coffee.

WAITRESS
You're always alone, aren't you?

MCCALL
Just like the peace and quiet.

The door to the diner jingles open, a tall man in a sharp black suit walks in. It's JOHN WICK (Keanu Reeves), he's weary, hair slicked back, a fading bruise on his cheek. He takes a seat at the counter.

MCCALL
(pointing to Wick)
He looks like he could use a piece of that pie, too.

The waitress nods, prepares a slice, and walks it over to Wick.

As Wick looks down at the pie, McCall slowly rises from his table, drops a bill onto the table, and approaches the counter, standing a few feet away from Wick.

MCCALL
(looking at Wick)
Tough night?

WICK, without looking up from his pie, chuckles lightly.

WICK
You could say that.

MCCALL
A man of your skills shouldn't be having "tough nights".

Wick finally looks up, meeting McCall's gaze.

WICK
(smirking)
And how would you know about my skills?

McCall smiles, nods towards the exit.

MCCALL
I've seen a thing or two in my time.

He then starts to walk towards the door. He pauses and looks back at Wick.

MCCALL
Be seeing you, Mr. Wick.

Wick watches as McCall exits the diner, leaving him in his thoughts.

FADE OUT.

TO BE CONTINUED...

I'm a 39 year old virgin single male Christian man, 6"2, bald but ok looking (I got 7-8.5 on photofeeler which actually surprised me), well travelled, level headed, etc but I am brown skinned. This is something many will not admit to but race plays a crucial factor. White women generally are not into brown or dark skin. People prefer their own race and that's just how it is. I have to accept that sadly but this is the major reason as to why I'm single. It's been 8 years since I was properly with someone and that only lasted a few months and the time before that I was in an abusive relationship for a year. 

It may not be too late to meet someone but I haven't even come close. I rarely even meet someone I want to pursue. The few I do aren't into me and the ones that are, I'm not into. There always seems to be something missing. I very rarely get a mutual match on hinge and other dating apps. Infact this year even the dates are drying up and I feel with age my options are getting less. I am worried about being left on the shelf and being alone for the rest of my life. I've tried to lower my standards but that's the worst possible strategy, pretending and forcing yourself to like someone you know you're not into.

I know many will point out that confidence is the real issue here. But is it that surprising, given my situation - never had intercourse, no relationship in all these years, all the women I pursue don't have interest in me...this has eaten away my confidence over time. Every day I'm losing hope. I'm still trying, still on the dating apps, still go out when I can and try and meet new people but my future just looks bleak the longer I live with no one to share my life with. 

I wish I could have had an arranged marriage or something but I don't know how that would work now. I do want to lose my virginity at some point in my life. It's something I haven't thought about for a while but when I do it's horrifying to think that I still haven't done this. I don't want to pay for it. 

I have full time work but it's not fulfilling at all. I have my own home, a nice car, my health for which of course I'm grateful but those are standard things and women have far higher expectations due to high entitlement. You can't just be an average man, low status man. I struggle to see hope in the future at this point. Many will tell me I wish I had your height or some of those things above which I am thankful for, however would anyone trade places with me - a real life 40 year old virgin....? Yeah I didn't think so... If I knew life would turn out this way, I would have chosen not have been born.
